info.name: FamilyOS API
info.description: Postman collection for FamilyOS API endpoints. After importing, set the environment variables (baseUrl and token) before making requests.

request.description: Check service health status
Get home page message
Get current authenticated user information
Get latest Gmail messages for current user
Get Gmail messages matching configured sender/subject allowlists
Read the persisted Gmail allowlist
Get Drive/DB storage sync status
Bootstrap JSON from Google Drive into PostgreSQL runtime data
Export PostgreSQL runtime data to Google Drive JSON
Get upcoming Google Calendar events for current user
Get recent Google Drive files for current user
Get Google Tasks items for current user
Get Google Contacts for current user
Generate LLM response. Provider can be 'gemini', 'openai', etc.

query.description: Test prompt to send to LLM

request.description: Test LLM endpoint with simple prompt
[Phase 3] Check health status of the default LLM provider (configured via LLM_DEFAULT_PROVIDER environment variable)
[Phase 3] Generate LLM response using default provider (systemPrompt and userPrompt required)
[Phase 3] Generate LLM response explicitly using Gemini provider
[Phase 3] Generate LLM response explicitly using Groq provider
[Phase 3] Generate LLM response explicitly using OpenRouter provider
Simple test endpoint

item.name: Rule Engine
item.description: Phase 4 – Rule Engine: Configurable decision-making engine

body.raw:

  "id": "doc-school-001",
  "sender": "Lincoln Elementary School",
  "subject": "Permission Slip Required",
  "content": "Please sign and return the permission slip for the field trip",
  "labels": [],
  "priority": "high",
  "source": "email"
}

request.description: Test SenderRule matching – School sender should be PROCESS (priority 95)

request.description: Shows the error shape when parsing or provider access fails.

Persists source document, extraction, and mapped domain actions with deterministic payload.

variable.description: Base URL for the API. Change port if using SERVER_PORT environment variable.
